candidates.name: Tony Evers
candidates.notes: 46th Governor of Wisconsin (2019-). Religion not publicly disclosed. Former state Superintendent of Public Instruction. Vetoed Republican-passed abortion bans, school choice expansions, and voter ID measures. NRA F. Will not seek third term. Lifelong educator. Euchre and bobblehead enthusiast.

profile.education: University of Wisconsin-Madison (BS, MS, PhD in Educational Administration)
profile.background: Career educator; science teacher, principal, and superintendent; Wisconsin Superintendent of Public Instruction (2009-2019); became 46th governor in 2019.

candidates.name: Ron Johnson
candidates.notes: Senior Senator from Wisconsin (2011-). Wisconsin Evangelical Lutheran Synod — one of only six senators of this denomination. Heritage Action lifetime 83%. NRA A — received $1.2M from NRA over career (15th highest). Businessman before politics. Strong Trump ally. Pro-life, pro-2A, skeptic of climate change and COVID vaccines. Deep religious convictions from parents.

profile.background: Manufacturing executive and CEO of PACUR, a polyester and plastics company. First elected to Senate in 2010 with no prior political experience. Chairman of Homeland Security Committee.
profile.office: 328 Hart Senate Office Building

candidates.name: Tammy Baldwin
candidates.notes: Junior Senator from Wisconsin (2013-). Baptized Episcopalian but identifies as religiously 'unaffiliated.' Heritage Action lifetime 6%. NRA F — NRA spent $771K against her. First openly gay U.S. Senator. First woman senator from Wisconsin. Reelected 2024. Consistently progressive across all categories. Co-authored Respect for Marriage Act.

profile.education: Smith College (BA Political Science/Mathematics), University of Wisconsin (JD)
profile.background: Attorney and former Wisconsin State Assembly member. First openly gay U.S. Senator. Served in U.S. House from 1999-2012 before winning Senate seat. Reelected in 2024.
